title: Agent identity and key management
description:
Persistent agent identity for clawsig. Today every `wrap` run generates an ephemeral DID — agents have no continuity across runs. Need stable identity for reputation, bounty binding, and fleet management.

Source: clawsig best-practices gap analysis (2026-02-21).

title: SIG-KEY-001: clawsig init --identity persistent key generation
description:
clawsig init should offer to generate a persistent Ed25519 key pair and store it at a well-known location (~/.clawsig/keys/<project>.jwk.json or .clawsig/identity.jwk.json).

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig init creates key file if missing
- clawsig wrap auto-discovers key file (env > project > global)
- DID remains stable across runs when key file exists
- Key file permissions set to 600
- Works on macOS + Linux

title: SIG-KEY-002: DID rotation with continuity proof
description:
When an agent rotates its key (compromise, expiry, fleet reorg), produce a signed rotation proof linking old DID to new DID so verifiers and reputation systems can follow the chain.

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig rotate-key generates new key + rotation proof
- Rotation proof is a SignedEnvelope with old key signing new DID
- clawverify validates rotation proofs
- Reputation/bounty bindings follow rotation chain

title: SIG-KEY-003: Fleet identity registry (multi-agent key management)
description:
For teams running multiple agents, provide a registry mapping agent names/roles to DIDs with key storage, discovery, and revocation.

Acceptance criteria:
- clawsig fleet add <name> generates and registers a key
- clawsig fleet list shows all registered agent DIDs
- clawsig fleet revoke <name> marks key as revoked
- Fleet registry stored at ~/.clawsig/fleet/ or project-level .clawsig/fleet/
- Revoked keys are rejected by clawverify

title: Clawsig Privacy Assurance v1
description:
Move Clawsig from provenance-only proofs toward privacy assurance: fail-closed egress, proxy-only model execution, sensitivity handling, processor policy, and reviewer-facing privacy posture reports.

Roadmap: docs/roadmaps/clawsig-privacy-v1/README.md

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #475
- Merged PR #476
- Merged PR #477
- Merged PR #478
- Merged PR #479
- Merged PR #480
- Merged PR #481

title: PRV-EGR-001: Fail-closed outbound allowlist for proofed runs
description:
Add deny-by-default outbound policy for proofed runs so only allowlisted destinations (Clawproxy + approved internal services) may be reached. Direct provider endpoints and arbitrary third-party egress must fail closed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Proofed mode defaults to deny-by-default outbound traffic
- Allowed destinations are configurable via policy
- Direct provider endpoints are rejected in proofed mode
- Blocked outbound attempts return deterministic reason codes
- Integration test proves unauthorized host egress is blocked

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #476

title: PRV-EGR-002: Proxy-only model execution in proofed mode
description:
Force proofed runs to route model traffic through Clawproxy only. If the proxy path is unavailable or a provider would be called directly, fail closed instead of silently downgrading privacy guarantees.

Acceptance criteria:
- Proofed mode routes Google/OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter traffic through Clawproxy only
- Direct provider calls fail closed in proofed mode
- Gateway-tier proofs are impossible without proxy mediation
- Regression tests cover at least one real proofed provider path

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #476

title: PRV-EGR-003: Signed egress policy receipt
description:
Emit a signed receipt describing the egress policy in force for the run: allowed destinations, whether direct providers were blocked, and whether any blocked attempts occurred.

Acceptance criteria:
- Proof bundle carries a signed egress policy receipt or canonical equivalent
- Receipt records policy version or hash
- Receipt records whether direct provider access was blocked
- Receipt records blocked attempt counts
- clawverify validates the receipt and fails closed on malformed policy evidence

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #479

title: PRV-RUN-001: Minimal proofed sandbox profile
description:
Define a minimal runtime profile for privacy-sensitive proofed runs with reduced ambient process/network noise so privacy telemetry is interpretable.

Acceptance criteria:
- A dedicated proofed sandbox/runtime profile exists
- Baseline process inventory is captured at run start
- Proofed mode documents the difference between dev mode and privacy-assurance mode
- Runtime profile reduces ambient browser/helper noise versus current ad hoc execution
- Fallback behavior is explicit when the profile cannot be activated

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #475

title: PRV-RUN-002: Runtime hygiene receipt + CLDD noise budget
description:
Convert raw runtime telemetry into a signed runtime hygiene receipt with clear verdicts: interpose active, baseline hash, unmediated/unmonitored counts, and whether the run stayed inside the acceptable noise budget.

Acceptance criteria:
- Proof bundle carries a signed runtime hygiene receipt
- Receipt includes sandbox/profile identity and baseline process metadata
- Raw CLDD signals are bucketed into good/caution/action verdicts
- clawsig prove can distinguish background noise from reviewer-action-required signals
- Regression tests cover at least one noisy-environment classification path

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #475

title: PRV-DLP-001: Pre-egress sensitivity classifier
description:
Classify outbound model payloads before egress for secrets, credentials, PII, customer-restricted content, and custom policy patterns.

Acceptance criteria:
- Classifier runs before outbound model requests in proofed mode
- Default rule set covers secrets, credentials, PII, and customer-defined patterns
- Classifier returns deterministic class/rule IDs for matched content
- Unknown/unsupported classifier states fail closed or require explicit override
- Tests cover positive and negative matches for at least secrets + PII

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #478

title: PRV-DLP-002: Block/redact/approval engine + data-handling receipt
description:
Add a policy action engine for sensitive classes: allow, redact, block, or require approval. Emit a signed data-handling receipt so reviewers can see what classes were found and what action was taken.

Acceptance criteria:
- Each sensitive class maps to allow/redact/block/require-approval
- Redacted payloads are the actual payloads sent to the provider
- Blocked payloads fail closed with deterministic reason codes
- Proof bundle carries a signed data-handling receipt with class list + action taken
- clawverify validates the data-handling receipt

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #478

title: PRV-POL-001: Processor policy engine + processor receipt
description:
Enforce approved provider/model/region/retention combinations for proofed runs and emit signed processor policy evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
- Policy supports provider/model allowlists
- Policy supports region and retention-profile constraints
- Disallowed processor routes fail closed in proofed mode
- Proof bundle carries a signed processor policy receipt
- clawsig prove surfaces the approved processor profile used for the run

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #477

title: PRV-UX-001: Privacy posture report in clawsig prove
description:
Upgrade clawsig prove to render privacy posture clearly: allowed processors used, blocked destinations, sensitive classes detected, actions taken, runtime hygiene, and reviewer action needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Terminal + HTML proof reports include a privacy posture section
- Report shows allowed processor(s), blocked egress attempts, sensitive classes, and data-handling actions
- Report distinguishes background noise from action-required privacy signals
- JSON output includes machine-readable privacy verdicts
- Sample proof report explains what is proven vs what is not proven

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #480

title: PRV-AUD-001: Privacy/compliance export pack
description:
Produce an auditor-facing export pack that bundles proof bundle evidence, policy receipts, and a clear statement of privacy claims and non-claims.

Acceptance criteria:
- Single command emits a privacy/compliance export pack
- Export pack includes proof bundle plus privacy policy receipts
- Export pack includes a human-readable what-is-proven / not-proven summary
- Artifact is stable enough to attach to security reviews and disputes
- Docs explain how reviewers should interpret the pack

Completion evidence:
- Merged PR #481

title: Clawsig Framework vNext
description:
Take Clawsig from privacy-v1 controls to a broader assurance framework: reliability cleanup, signed policy bundles, runner attestation, marketplace enforcement, reviewer/dispute UX, richer DLP, cross-runtime parity, and transparency/revocation.

Roadmap: docs/roadmaps/clawsig-framework-vnext/README.md
notes: Clawsig Framework vNext complete. Reliability lane merged via PR #484, signed-policy lane via PR #485, runner-measurement lane via PR #487, richer DLP taxonomy lane via PR #489, policy-bound approval receipts lane via PR #490, runner attestation receipt lane via PR #492, DLP simulation lane via PR #493, attested trust-tier uplift lane via PR #495, attestation posture lane via PR #497, marketplace assurance requirements lane via PR #498, reviewer/export-pack viewer lane via PR #500, marketplace submission gating lane via PR #501, payout/review policy lane via PR #503, side-by-side run comparison lane via PR #504, Python proofed adapter parity lane via PR #506, reviewer signoff receipt lane via PR #507, reviewer signoff hardening follow-up via PR #508, browser proofed adapter parity lane via PR #510, transparency receipt anchor lane via PR #511, and trust-root revocation lane via PR #513. 24/24 child stories shipped as of 2026-03-21T17:31:20Z.

title: AF2-REL-001: Repair clawverify-cli submit/test debt
description:
Fix the existing work-submit parser/transform debt so the canonical clawverify-cli test suite is structurally runnable again.

Acceptance criteria:
- packages/clawverify-cli/test/work-submit.test.ts parses and runs in CI
- No duplicate declarations / truncated file syntax remains
- Regression coverage preserves current submit-path behavior
- Release/test guardrails fail closed when the suite regresses again
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 reliability lane. Merged via PR #484 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/484). Reliability guardrails and targeted tests green. 2026-03-20T23:15:15Z.

title: AF2-REL-002: Deterministic SIGTERM proof finalization
description:
Make wrap shutdown/finalization deterministic so proof bundles are still emitted when the parent or child exits under SIGTERM scenarios.

Acceptance criteria:
- wrap-sigterm tests are green
- Proof bundle emission survives child-normal-exit and parent-SIGTERM paths
- Run summary / bundle writing is atomic enough to avoid partial artifacts
- No regression in normal wrap success/failure reporting
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 reliability lane. Merged via PR #484 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/484). Reliability guardrails and targeted tests green. 2026-03-20T23:15:15Z.

title: AF2-REL-003: Canonical receipt-only proof bundles
description:
Remove unsigned synthetic/fallback receipt leakage from canonical proof-bundle paths so offline verification only sees verifier-supported evidence shapes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Canonical proof bundles do not mix unsigned synthetic observations into payload.receipts
- Fallback/internal observations live in explicit non-canonical metadata channels or are omitted
- Offline verify/prove/export paths agree on what counts as canonical evidence
- Regression tests cover the previously confusing fallback/genealogy cases
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 reliability lane. Merged via PR #484 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/484). Reliability guardrails and targeted tests green. 2026-03-20T23:15:15Z.

title: AF2-REL-004: Release guardrail for green proof-production paths
description:
Close the loop on framework reliability with CI/release guardrails that require canonical proof-production paths to stay green before release.

Acceptance criteria:
- Guardrail workflow fails when submit/wrap/prove critical paths regress
- Guardrail covers at least work-submit, wrap-sigterm, and proof-bundle compatibility
- Failure output points directly to broken proof-production surfaces
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 reliability lane. Merged via PR #484 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/484). Reliability guardrails and targeted tests green. 2026-03-20T23:15:15Z.

title: AF2-POL-001: Signed policy bundle schema + envelope
description:
Define canonical signed policy bundles so assurance policy stops living as ambient runtime config and becomes a first-class verifiable artifact.

Acceptance criteria:
- Canonical policy bundle payload + envelope schemas exist
- Policy bundle carries policy hash, scope, issuer, issued_at, and additive policy fields
- Verifier/CLI can parse and validate signed policy bundles
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 signed-policy lane. Merged via PR #485 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/485). Includes reviewer and CI follow-up fixes for fail-closed policy binding + guardrail coverage. 2026-03-20T23:58:45Z.

title: AF2-POL-002: Hierarchical policy resolution
description:
Resolve assurance policy deterministically across org/project/task/exception scopes and bind the effective policy into proofed runs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Effective policy resolution order is deterministic and documented
- Project/task exceptions are explicit and auditable
- Proofed runs materialize a single effective policy snapshot/hash
- Regression tests cover conflict/override behavior fail-closed
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 signed-policy lane. Merged via PR #485 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/485). Includes reviewer and CI follow-up fixes for fail-closed policy binding + guardrail coverage. 2026-03-20T23:58:45Z.

title: AF2-POL-003: Signed approval receipts
description:
Replace ad hoc approval tokens with signed approval receipts so exception paths are auditable and verifier-usable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Approval is represented as a signed receipt, not a raw shared-secret token
- Receipts bind approver identity, scope, policy hash, expiry, and decision
- Require-approval flows fail closed without a valid signed approval receipt
notes: Implemented in the Wave 2 policy-controls lane. Merged via PR #490 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/490). Proofed approval flow now uses signed human approval receipts with approver DID allowlists and fail-closed verification. 2026-03-21T01:52:26Z.

title: AF2-POL-004: Policy-hash-bound verification
description:
Bind proof receipts and verifier outcomes to the canonical effective policy hash so claims are about a signed policy, not ambient env state.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verifier checks effective policy hash where policy-bound receipts require it
- prove/export surfaces the active policy hash/profile coherently
- Policy mismatches fail closed with deterministic reason codes
notes: Implemented in the Wave 2 policy-controls lane. Merged via PR #490 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/490). Data-handling approval evidence is now explicitly bound to the effective signed policy hash in runtime, offline verifier, and service verifier paths. 2026-03-21T01:52:26Z.

title: AF2-ATT-001: Runner measurement manifest
description:
Establish a measurable runner identity surface (binary/runtime manifest, loaded policy/runtime fingerprint) that later attestations can sign.

Acceptance criteria:
- Runner measurement manifest captures binary/runtime/material inputs deterministically
- Manifest is stable enough to hash/sign
- Manifest generation works in proofed runs without breaking existing receipts
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 runner-measurement lane. Merged via PR #487 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/487). Deterministic proofed-run runner measurement metadata now binds into proof bundles and is validated fail-closed offline and service-side. 2026-03-21T00:27:28Z.

title: AF2-ATT-002: Signed runner attestation receipt
description:
Emit a signed attestation receipt that binds runner measurement, effective policy hash, and key binary/runtime hashes to the run.

Acceptance criteria:
- Attestation receipt binds measurement manifest + effective policy hash
- Verifier validates signature and binding fail-closed
- Receipt schema and tests cover malformed/forged attestation paths
notes: Implemented in the Wave 3 attestation lane. Merged via PR #492 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/492). Proofed runs now emit signed runner attestation receipts bound to runner measurement, event/run anchors, and effective signed policy hash. 2026-03-21T02:28:29Z.

title: AF2-ATT-003: Attested trust-tier uplift
description:
Teach the verifier and framework surfaces to distinguish attested runs from merely observed runs, with explicit trust-tier uplift rules.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verifier outputs an attested assurance tier when attestation is valid
- Invalid/missing attestation does not accidentally uplift trust
- Reason codes explain why an attested tier was or was not granted
notes: Implemented in the Wave 4 attestation lane. Merged via PR #495 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/495). Attested trust-tier uplift now requires valid runner attestation and emits deterministic attested-tier reason codes. 2026-03-21T03:03:21Z.

title: AF2-ATT-004: Attestation posture in prove/export
description:
Surface attestation state coherently in prove/export/reporting so reviewers can see whether the runner itself was measured and policy-bound.

Acceptance criteria:
- prove/export report attested vs non-attested posture clearly
- What-is-proven / not-proven boundaries mention runner attestation honestly
- HTML/export pack include attestation posture when present
notes: Implemented in the Wave 5 attestation posture lane. Merged via PR #497 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/497). `clawsig prove` / export surfaces now report attested vs non-attested posture conservatively with deterministic reason codes and extracted runner-attestation evidence. 2026-03-21T04:00:27Z.

title: AF2-MKT-001: Bounty-level assurance requirements
description:
Let marketplaces/bounties require specific assurance/privacy posture levels, processors, and approval policies before work is accepted.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bounty/task contract can declare required assurance policy/tier
- Requirements are machine-readable and shown to workers before submission
- Unsupported requirement sets fail closed rather than silently downgrade
notes: Implemented in the Wave 5 marketplace assurance-requirements lane. Merged via PR #498 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/498). Bounties/tasks now declare strict machine-readable assurance requirements, workers see them before acceptance/submission, and unsupported sets fail closed. 2026-03-21T04:07:14Z.

title: AF2-MKT-002: Submission gating on required evidence
description:
Reject or quarantine submissions that do not satisfy bounty-required evidence/tiers instead of relying on manual reviewer memory.

Acceptance criteria:
- Submission path enforces required assurance/privacy evidence
- Auto-rejection/quarantine reason codes are deterministic
- Reviewer UI can see exactly which required evidence was missing/invalid
notes: Implemented in the Wave 6 marketplace submission-gating lane. Merged via PR #501 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/501). Submissions now enforce required assurance evidence at submit time with deterministic reject/quarantine reason codes and post-verification reconciliation. 2026-03-21T04:38:44Z.

title: AF2-MKT-003: Payout and review policy by assurance tier
description:
Use assurance tiers and approval evidence to drive review routing, payout readiness, and escalation rules.

Acceptance criteria:
- Marketplace can route/pause payouts based on assurance tier and approval evidence
- Reviewer workflows expose why a payout is gated
- Regression tests cover both satisfied and blocked payout paths
notes: Implemented in the Wave 7 payout/review policy lane. Merged via PR #503 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/503). Payout/readiness and review routing now fail closed on assurance/proof/approval blockers with deterministic policy reason codes in submission surfaces. 2026-03-21T05:02:19Z.

title: AF2-REV-001: Hosted reviewer/export-pack viewer
description:
Turn export packs into a portable hosted reviewer surface so buyers/auditors can inspect runs without raw repo/file spelunking.

Acceptance criteria:
- Export pack can be rendered in a portable hosted/local viewer
- Viewer aligns with prove/export language and privacy posture sections
- Viewer supports direct artifact navigation without leaking creator-machine paths
notes: Implemented in the Wave 6 reviewer viewer lane. Merged via PR #500 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/500). Export packs now include a portable local/hosted reviewer viewer and pre-rendered report HTML with pack-local artifact navigation. 2026-03-21T04:26:01Z.

title: AF2-REV-002: Side-by-side run comparison
description:
Allow reviewers to compare two runs and focus on privacy/assurance differences rather than manually diffing JSON.

Acceptance criteria:
- Comparison view highlights evidence/tier/policy/processor/privacy deltas
- Blocked attempts, approvals, and redactions are diffed coherently
- Comparison artifacts can be exported/shared
notes: Implemented in the Wave 7 side-by-side comparison lane. Merged via PR #504 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/504). `clawsig prove --compare-with` now emits portable pack-local comparison artifacts and viewer/report deltas across assurance, evidence, policy, processor, and privacy categories. 2026-03-21T05:06:45Z.

title: AF2-REV-003: Reviewer signoff receipt + dispute-note binding
description:
Bind reviewer signoff and dispute notes into signed receipts so review outcomes themselves become auditable artifacts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reviewer signoff receipt captures reviewer identity, decision, timestamp, and bound run/export pack
- Dispute notes/evidence references are tied to that signoff
- Verifier/viewer can surface signoff/dispute state
notes: Implemented in the Wave 8 reviewer-signoff lane. Feature merged via PR #507 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/507) and reporting/verifier-alignment hardening landed in PR #508 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/508). Reviewer signoff receipts and dispute-note bindings are now signed, fail-closed, and surfaced coherently in verifier/viewer outputs. 2026-03-21T10:17:37Z.

title: AF2-DLP-001: Richer sensitivity taxonomy + custom rules
description:
Expand sensitivity handling beyond the minimal v1 classes with org-defined patterns and a more usable class taxonomy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Policy supports richer built-in classes and custom rule definitions
- Rule/class IDs remain deterministic and signed-receipt compatible
- False-positive/false-negative paths are tested explicitly
notes: Implemented in the Wave 2 DLP lane. Merged via PR #489 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/489). Added richer deterministic sensitivity classes plus custom DLP rule support with policy-bound receipt evidence. 2026-03-21T01:00:03Z.

title: AF2-DLP-002: Structured redaction + simulation mode
description:
Support structured redaction and a simulation/preview mode so teams can tune policy without flying blind.

Acceptance criteria:
- Structured fields can be redacted deterministically
- Simulation mode reports what would have been blocked/redacted without allowing silent bypass in proofed mode
- Receipts distinguish simulated vs enforced actions clearly
notes: Implemented in the Wave 3 DLP lane. Merged via PR #493 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/493). Added deterministic structured redaction evidence and simulation-mode DLP receipts without weakening enforced fail-closed proofed mode. 2026-03-21T02:36:18Z.

title: AF2-XRT-001: Python proofed adapter parity
description:
Bring the proofed policy/receipt/attestation surface to Python agent runs so the framework is not Node-only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Python proofed adapter routes through the same assurance policy model
- Core receipts/evidence surfaces remain verifier-compatible
- At least one end-to-end Python proofed path is tested
notes: Implemented in the Wave 8 Python parity lane. Merged via PR #506 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/506). Proofed Python runs now share the same policy/receipt/attestation model as Node proofed runs, with fail-closed bypass-flag handling and end-to-end verifier-compatible coverage. 2026-03-21T05:34:35Z.

title: AF2-XRT-002: Browser/automation proofed adapter parity
description:
Extend assurance controls to browser/automation runners so reviewer trust does not depend on a single runtime family.

Acceptance criteria:
- Browser automation runner can emit compatible assurance/privacy evidence
- Policy and attestation surfaces remain coherent across runtimes
- At least one browser-run proof path is tested end-to-end
notes: Implemented in the Wave 9 browser-parity lane. Merged via PR #510 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/510). Browser-named proofed runs now enforce clawsig-managed proxy mediation fail-closed, preserve policy/attestation/evidence parity, and include end-to-end browser proof-path coverage. 2026-03-21T10:59:35Z.

title: AF2-TRN-001: Transparency log for assurance receipts
description:
Add transparency-log style anchoring for policy, attestation, and reviewer receipts so retroactive tampering is harder to hide.

Acceptance criteria:
- Selected assurance receipts can be anchored in a transparency log
- Verifier can check inclusion/consistency evidence when present
- Log integration remains optional/additive for non-logged runs
notes: Implemented in the Wave 9 transparency-log lane. Merged via PR #511 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/511). Policy, runner attestation, and reviewer receipts can now carry optional transparency anchors with fail-closed inclusion/consistency verification while leaving non-logged runs valid. 2026-03-21T11:06:27Z.

title: AF2-TRN-002: Revocation for runner/policy/reviewer keys
description:
Provide revocation semantics for compromised or retired runner, policy-issuer, and reviewer keys so old trust roots can be withdrawn cleanly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Revocation artifacts exist for runner/policy/reviewer identities
- Verifier rejects revoked trust roots deterministically
- Review/export surfaces explain revocation-driven failures
notes: Implemented in the Wave 10 revocation lane. Merged via PR #513 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/513). Policy-issuer, runner, and reviewer trust roots can now be revoked deterministically, verifier surfaces reject revoked roots fail-closed, and prove/export outputs explain revocation-driven failures conservatively. 2026-03-21T17:31:20Z.

title: Clawcompiler Runtime v1
description:
Build the first deployed Clawcompiler runtime: deterministic compilation from already-verified proof artifacts into signed, portable, auditor-consumable compiled evidence.

Roadmap: docs/roadmaps/clawcompiler-runtime-v1/README.md
notes: Wave 1 foundations shipped via PR #516, closing monorepo-cec2.1/.2/.3. Wave 2 authoritative runtime shipped via PR #518, closing monorepo-cec2.4/.5/.6. Wave 3 assurance pack shipped via PR #520, closing monorepo-cec2.7/.8. Wave 4 product surfaces shipped via PRs #522/#523, closing monorepo-cec2.9/.10. Clawcompiler Runtime v1 is now complete at 10/10 shipped stories. 2026-03-22T01:09:28Z.

title: CEC-RT-001: Verified-evidence ingest contract
description:
Define the authoritative compile input contract for already-verified proof bundles, export packs, and verifier results so compiled evidence never depends on mutable or unauthenticated inputs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Define deterministic compile input shapes for verified evidence
- Reject unknown schema/version/reference shapes fail-closed
- Require upstream evidence to be verified before authoritative compilation proceeds
- Canonicalize evidence references by content hash
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 foundations lane. Merged via PR #516 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/516). Authoritative compiler inputs now require verifier-backed evidence facts, reject malformed compile inputs fail-closed, and canonicalize authoritative bundle-hash context before compilation. 2026-03-21T22:33:52Z.

title: CEC-RT-002: Deterministic control-pack runtime
description:
Implement the bounded deterministic rule runtime that evaluates control packs with stable ordering and explicit reason-code semantics.

Acceptance criteria:
- Stable rule ordering and bounded execution
- Deterministic control outcomes and reason codes
- No probabilistic or free-form decisions at authoritative boundaries
- Fail closed on unsupported operators or malformed inputs
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 foundations lane. Merged via PR #516 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/516). Clawcompiler now has an explicit deterministic Wave-1 control-pack runtime in clawverify-core with stable control evaluation ordering, deterministic reason codes, and explicit missing-evidence outcomes. 2026-03-21T22:33:52Z.

title: CEC-RT-003: Fail-closed compiler state machine
description:
Define deterministic compiler precedence so invalid or incomplete upstream evidence halts compilation rather than being narrated away.

Acceptance criteria:
- INVALID upstream evidence halts authoritative compilation fail-closed
- Missing required evidence yields explicit deterministic outcomes
- Compiler precedence is documented and stable
- Upstream reason codes are preserved or mapped deterministically
notes: Implemented in the Wave 1 foundations lane. Merged via PR #516 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/516). Upstream INVALID verification now halts authoritative compilation fail-closed with preserved/mapped reason codes, malformed compiler inputs reject deterministically, and ledger/CLI surfaces expose those fail-closed outcomes instead of placeholder success. 2026-03-21T22:33:52Z.

title: CEC-RT-004: Deterministic compiled report generation
description:
Generate byte-stable authoritative compiled reports whose hashes are reproducible for identical evidence and rules.

Acceptance criteria:
- Stable ordering and canonical serialization inputs
- Deterministic authoritative results/content hashes
- Identical inputs + rules yield byte-identical output
- Conformance coverage proves determinism across reruns
notes: Implemented in the Wave 2 authoritative-runtime lane. Merged via PR #518 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/518). Added deterministic compiled-report generation with stable control ordering, canonical matrix hashing, deterministic evidence refs, and byte-stable compiled report IDs. 2026-03-21T23:36:05Z.

title: CEC-RT-005: Signed compiler service/runtime
description:
Add the first runtime/service that emits signed compiled reports rather than schema-only fixtures.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add first `services/clawcompiler` runtime/service scaffold or equivalent compile endpoint
- Authoritative compile outputs are wrapped in signed compiler envelopes
- Compiler signer identity is explicit and allowlistable
- Runtime does not emit unsigned authoritative reports
notes: Implemented in the Wave 2 authoritative-runtime lane. Merged via PR #518 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/518). Added signer-bound compiled-report envelopes plus a narrow compiler endpoint/runtime surface for canonicalize-and-sign compiled report flows. 2026-03-21T23:36:05Z.

title: CEC-RT-006: Compiled-report verification in CLI/service
description:
Add offline and hosted verification for compiled report envelopes so portability does not depend on trusting the compiler blindly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Verification support for compiled report envelopes in CLI and/or service
- Recompute matrix/report hashes and validate signer identity
- Deterministic invalid codes for signature/hash/ref violations
- Offline verification works without network requirements
notes: Implemented in the Wave 2 authoritative-runtime lane. Merged via PR #518 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/518). Added offline and hosted compiled-report verification with deterministic signature, hash, ref, and schema failure reason codes. 2026-03-21T23:36:05Z.

title: CEC-RT-007: AI execution assurance control pack
description:
Ship the first real control pack that maps Clawsig/Clawverify facts into an execution-assurance matrix rather than a generic empty compiler shell.

Acceptance criteria:
- Versioned AI execution assurance pack using existing proof facts
- Covers policy, egress, DLP/privacy, attestation, and reviewer/signoff style controls where evidence exists
- Each control result includes deterministic reason codes and evidence links
- No control outcome overstates proof strength beyond upstream verifier facts
notes: Implemented in the Wave 3 assurance-pack lane. Merged via PR #520 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/520). Added the first narrow, evidence-grounded AI execution assurance pack (`CLAW_AI_EXECUTION_ASSURANCE_V1` / `claw.ai_execution_assurance.v1`) with deterministic controls for policy, approvals, egress, DLP/privacy, attestation posture, and reviewer signoff evidence. 2026-03-22T00:30:21Z.

title: CEC-RT-008: Waiver and compensating-control semantics
description:
Add signed waiver semantics so human exceptions are explicit, bounded, and never silently promote failing controls to PASS.

Acceptance criteria:
- Signed waivers and compensating-control markers represented as compiler inputs
- Waivers can only degrade FAIL to PARTIAL, never promote to PASS
- Residual markers emit deterministically
- Waiver binding, expiry, and scope are fail-closed validated
notes: Implemented in the Wave 3 assurance-pack lane. Merged via PR #520 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/520). Added signed waiver / compensating-control semantics with fail-closed validation, deterministic residual markers, and conservative FAIL->PARTIAL downgrade rules only. 2026-03-22T00:30:21Z.

title: CEC-RT-009: Export-pack and viewer integration
description:
Surface compiled evidence reports inside existing export/viewer flows so the compiler becomes part of the actual auditor handoff.

Acceptance criteria:
- Compiled authoritative reports can be included in export packs or equivalent handoff artifacts
- Viewer/report surfaces link compiled outcomes back to source evidence
- Authoritative vs non-authoritative surfaces remain distinct
- Integration remains additive and does not break existing proof-pack workflows
notes: Implemented in the Wave 4 export/viewer lane. Merged via PR #523 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/523). Compiled evidence reports now surface in export packs, prove reports, and viewer navigation with deterministic pack-local artifact links while preserving authoritative vs non-authoritative boundaries. 2026-03-22T01:09:28Z.

title: CEC-RT-010: Non-authoritative narrative runtime
description:
Add the optional narrative plane so compiled evidence is explainable without allowing prose to override authoritative outcomes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Runtime emits optional narrative plane with `authoritative:false` and fixed disclaimer
- Narrative plane includes stable hash pin to authoritative matrix/report
- Policy gates can disable narrative generation entirely
- Narrative tamper or mismatch fails closed and cannot change authoritative results
notes: Implemented in the Wave 4 narrative-runtime lane. Merged via PR #522 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/522). Compiled reports now support an optional non-authoritative narrative plane with fixed disclaimer, authoritative hash bindings, policy/runtime disable gates, and fail-closed validation for narrative tamper or mismatch. 2026-03-22T01:09:28Z.

title: Clawbounties Assurance Review v1

Adopt Clawcompiler Runtime v1 compiled evidence outputs inside live Clawbounties submission/reviewer workflows.

notes: Compiled evidence is now verified and normalized at ingest with proof-bundle hash binding, authoritative-vs-narrative boundary handling, and deterministic reviewer summary persistence. notes: Submission detail/list APIs now surface compact reviewer-facing compiled evidence posture, including authoritative-vs-narrative boundaries and key non-pass controls. Implemented in Wave 1 and merged via PR #527 (https://github.com/clawbureau/clawbureau/pull/527). 2026-03-22T02:09:22Z.
